(French pronunciation: [[sɛ̃ sipʁijɛ̃]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:LL-Q150_(fra)-Fabricio_Cardenas_(Culex)-Saint-Cyprien.wav "File:LL-Q150 (fra)-Fabricio Cardenas (Culex)-Saint-Cyprien.wav")) is a parish in the [Les Etchemins Regional County Municipality](/wiki/Les_Etchemins_Regional_County_Municipality "Les Etchemins Regional County Municipality") in [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec"), [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada"). It is part of the [Chaudière-Appalaches](/wiki/Chaudi%C3%A8re-Appalaches "Chaudière-Appalaches") region and the population is 490 as of 2016. It is named after [Christian](/wiki/Christianity "Christianity") [martyr](/wiki/Martyr "Martyr") [Cyprian](/wiki/Cyprian "Cyprian").

**Saint-Cyprien** (French pronunciation: [[sɛ̃ sipʁijɛ̃]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:LL-Q150_(fra)-Fabricio_Cardenas_(Culex)-Saint-Cyprien.wav "File:LL-Q150 (fra)-Fabricio Cardenas (Culex)-Saint-Cyprien.wav")) is a parish in the [Les Etchemins Regional County Municipality](/wiki/Les_Etchemins_Regional_County_Municipality "Les Etchemins Regional County Municipality") in [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec"), [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada"). It is part of the [Chaudière-Appalaches](/wiki/Chaudi%C3%A8re-Appalaches "Chaudière-Appalaches") region and the population is 490 as of 2016. It is named after [Christian](/wiki/Christianity "Christianity") [martyr](/wiki/Martyr "Martyr") [Cyprian](/wiki/Cyprian "Cyprian").

Saint-Cyprien lies on the [Canada–United States border](/wiki/Canada%E2%80%93United_States_border "Canada–United States border").

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint-Cyprien,_Chaudi%C3%A8re-Appalaches&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Demographics")]

In the [2021 Census of Population](/wiki/2021_Canadian_census "2021 Canadian census") conducted by [Statistics Canada](/wiki/Statistics_Canada "Statistics Canada"), Saint-Cyprien had a population of 474 living in 238 of its 290 total private dwellings, a change of -3.3% from its 2016 population of 490. With a land area of 93.48 km2 (36.09 sq mi), it had a population density of 5.1/km2 (13.1/sq mi) in 2021.[[4]](#cite_note-2021census-4)

Population trend:[[5]](#cite_note-5)

* Population in 2016: 490 (2011 to 2016 population change: -10.6%)
* Population in 2011: 548
* Population in 2006: 630
* Population in 2001: 603
* Population in 1996: 617
* Population in 1991: 664
* Population in 1986: 769
* Population in 1981: 838
* Population in 1976: 776
* Population in 1971: 905
* Population in 1966: 1,056
* Population in 1961: 1,101
* Population in 1956: 1,104
* Population in 1951: 1,058
* Population in 1941: 950
* Population in 1931: 626
* Population in 1921: 444

The [South Shore Protestant Regional School Board](/wiki/South_Shore_Protestant_Regional_School_Board "South Shore Protestant Regional School Board") previously served the municipality.[[6]](#cite_note-6)
